key_insight: A multihead physics-informed neural network can learn a low-dimensional latent manifold that summarizes families of PDE solutions, revealing robust principal components and frequency patterns beyond the initial condition.
engineering_relevance: This provides a reusable way to compress, compare, and analyze PDE solution spaces for simulation, surrogate modeling, and uncertainty-aware scientific computing.
method: Shared PINN body learns a latent embedding of the solution family.
Linear output heads reconstruct individual solutions for different initial conditions.
A head-orthogonalization penalty reduces degeneracy and stabilizes the latent spectrum.
evidence_signal: On the 1D viscous Burgers equation, a latent dimension of 20 compressed the learned space so that only 2-4 principal components captured about 95% of variance and 4-7 captured about 99%, with similar behavior for heat and wave equations.
watch_item: The latent representation is only identifiable up to a change of basis, so interpretation should rely on basis-invariant observables such as the spectral and Fourier-shell profiles.
paper_title: Physics-Informed Neural Embeddings of PDE Solution Families
